Born-red tested on real `LifeRecord`/`SilentStatus` inputs (`atk_fused_classify_input3.rs`, 6 tests) — the `Obsolete` test proves the trajectory actually fires `Drift` through the wiring (a blind axis would abstain to `RouteToHuman`). (Building the curation CLI command that DRIVES this pipeline end-to-end is a separate, larger unit — flagged to the lead, not assumed here.)
- **The `recall-Drift + Dormant` cell is HELD under a live design-Q, NOT silently resolved.** This ADR's INPUT-3 paragraph (above, ratify-context) says recall-`Drift` ⇒ `Evaded`; the research wiring note + the discriminator docstring concur. BUT the MERGED binding fusion contract (`atk_adwin4_virtual_drift_stays_dormant_never_forgets`, green on 0.6-dev) asserts `recall-Drift + Dormant ⇒ Dormant` (virtual-drift KEEP). SAME input, OPPOSITE verdict + curation action, both ratified — the un-named crux is whether a recall-drop on a shape-present/no-near-miss class is CHURN (corpus shrank → KEEP) or loud EVASION (defect escaping the static sensor → broaden), which a single recall `DriftVerdict` cannot itself distinguish. Wiring `Evaded` would RED a merged contract; wiring `Dormant` ignores INPUT-3. RESOLUTION DEFERRED to a first-principles ruling (it is an ADR-merit call, and switching to `Evaded` means AMENDING a merged binding contract — an adversarial-review call). As-built, `fused_classify` leaves the cell at the MERGED default (`Dormant`, via `fuse_channels`); the born-red INPUT-3 test asserts only the safety FLOOR both readings agree on for that cell (NEVER `Obsolete`), so it survives EITHER ruling without a silent re-bless. Once ruled, the contested cell is a one-line change in `fuse_channels` (+ amend `atk_adwin4` if it flips to `Evaded`).

Whole crate green (385 lib + 13 detector + 12 fusion-contract + 6 fused-classify + all siblings, 0 failed), clippy `--all-targets -D warnings` clean, fmt clean.

---

## ADR-065 Amendment 1 (2026-06-18, ruled on the v06 extend→ratify wave) — the recall-Drift + Dormant fusion cell is UNDECIDABLE on a denominator-free rate: RouteToHuman (the third conservatism-join cell), not Dormant/KEEP

**Status**: **RATIFIED** (v06 extend→ratify wave). Append-only; the §Decision (the floor→full seam + the real/virtual fusion + the conservatism-JOIN) stands as amended here.

**What the original ADR-065 §real/virtual-fusion was silent on (the ratified-vs-ratified conflict the ADWIN build surfaced).** ADR-065's fusion table listed `recall-drop + shape PRESENT + no near-miss = VIRTUAL drift (churn), KEEP` (⇒ `ClassVerdict::Dormant`). The build's `fuse_channels` (`antigen/src/learn/adwin.rs`) implemented exactly that, and the adversary's merged-binding-fusion ATK (`antigen/tests/atk_adwin_fusion_conservatism_join.rs`, table row "Drift recall-drop + Dormant ⇒ Dormant → Hold (VIRTUAL drift)") pinned it green. But the SAME cell was reserved as **Evaded (ReArm)** by ADR-065's INPUT-3 framing + the research note (a loud recall-drop on a still-present shape IS the evasion the streamless near-miss axis is blind to). Same input, opposite verdict AND opposite curation action, both ratified. The builder correctly refused to silently resolve it.

**The CODE-TRUE finding that resolves it (the first principle neither artifact named).** A recall-`Drift` is a drop in a **denominator-free RATE**, and the cluster-size denominator is **not recorded** anywhere in the substrate the detector reads (CODE-TRUE on `0.6-dev` / HEAD at ruling time):

- `Affinity { recall: f64, precision: f64 }` (`antigen/src/learn/affinity.rs`) — both fields are RATES in `[0,1]`. `recall` = "the FRACTION of defect-cluster members the draft matches"; the cluster-size is divided out at construction and discarded.
- `LifeEvent::Scored(Affinity)` (`antigen/src/learn/life_record.rs`) carries ONLY the 2-vector; `score_trajectory() -> Vec<Affinity>` is pure rates. There is no `cluster_size` / `corpus_size` / count field anywhere in the life-record.

Therefore a recall-rate drop (e.g. 0.9→0.4) is **genuinely UNDECIDABLE at a snapshot** between two causes the rate cannot distinguish:

- **CHURN** — the cluster shrank (denominator moved); the draft still binds all it can. The rate fell because the denominator changed. ⇒ KEEP (Dormant).
- **EVASION** — the cluster stayed; the defect mutated; the draft binds fewer members (numerator moved). ⇒ ReArm (Evaded).

The original code's comment ASSERTS the churn cause ("the recall dropped because the corpus churned, NOT because the defect mutated") — a **denominator claim it cannot make**. The INPUT-3 framing assumes the evasion cause — the opposite denominator claim. **Neither is justified on a denominator-free rate; each silently guesses one of two unverifiable causes.**

**The decision (what this amendment rules).** `recall-Drift + Dormant ⇒ ClassVerdict::RouteToHuman (HOLD)`. This is NOT a tie-break between two reasonable verdicts — it is the recognition that the cell has **no determinate verdict on the recorded substrate**, so the only honest action over an undecidable cause is to escalate (ADR-057's conservative default). The cell **JOINS the conservatism-JOIN's RouteToHuman family as the THIRD "a channel cannot decide" cell**, alongside the two ADR-065 already has:

- `UnderPowered` (ANY silent) ⇒ RouteToHuman — the ADWIN channel is blind.
- (ANY drift) `Indeterminate` ⇒ RouteToHuman — the bit-3 channel is blind.
- **(NEW) `recall-Drift` + `Dormant` ⇒ RouteToHuman — the DENOMINATOR channel is blind** (the rate discarded the count that would distinguish churn from evasion).

**Why RouteToHuman DOMINATES (not merely a safe default).** It neither rots (as a wrong auto-KEEP would — a silently-obsolete class kept forever) nor guesses (as a wrong auto-ReArm would), AND it preserves ADWIN's whole value: it **surfaces the drift the streamless near-miss axis was blind to** (an auto-KEEP would ignore the loud signal entirely, defeating the detector's purpose). The action-over-silence asymmetry (a wrong-ReArm self-corrects via the self-tolerance gate + the precision axis; a wrong-KEEP rots) is the fallback IF RouteToHuman were unavailable; at v0.6 the ratification interface exists (ADR-051), so the dominating option is available.

**The do-later that DISSOLVES this cell (the reserved future, named so a builder sees the cell is RouteToHuman *because the signal is missing*, not forever).** Record the denominator: a `Scored { affinity, cluster_size }` (or a sibling matched-count) field on the life-record event makes the cause decidable — `recall-drop + STABLE denominator = EVASION (⇒ Evaded/ReArm)`; `recall-drop + SHRINKING denominator = CHURN (⇒ Dormant/KEEP)` — and the cell **SPLITS back into both original verdicts**, retiring RouteToHuman for it. A future amendment makes that split when the denominator is recorded.

**The code + ATK changes this amendment ratifies (adversary applied, supersede-not-erase; commit `8d7e17c` on `amend-routetohuman`, merged as this commit).**

- `fuse_channels` (`adwin.rs`): the recall-`Drift` + `Dormant` arm yields `ClassVerdict::RouteToHuman` (was the `Dormant` passthrough via `_ => bit3`). Explicit arm added before the passthrough. Comment changed from "VIRTUAL drift, KEEP" to "UNDECIDABLE (churn vs evasion) on a denominator-free rate — escalate (ADR-057), the third conservatism-join cell; a recorded denominator would split it."
- The fusion-table doc-comment (`adwin.rs`, the `///` table preceding `fuse_channels`): the `Dormant` row flips from `Dormant (VIRTUAL drift, KEEP)` to `RouteToHuman (UNDECIDABLE cause — third conservatism-join cell)`. The conservatism-join now has THREE RouteToHuman rows.
- The conservatism-join ATK (`atk_adwin_fusion_conservatism_join.rs`): `atk_adwin4` renamed to `atk_adwin4_recall_drift_plus_dormant_routes_to_human`; assertion flipped from `ClassVerdict::Dormant` to `ClassVerdict::RouteToHuman`; doc-comment updated to name the denominator-free root cause and the RESERVE.

**Supersedes.** The Build-time ratification #3 entry above ("the `recall-Drift + Dormant` cell is HELD") — the cell is no longer held; it is ruled here. The build-time ratification #2 entry ("recall-`Drift` + `Dormant` ⇒ `Dormant`, the virtual-drift KEEP cell") — superseded in the same way; the code that implemented it is amended by this commit.

**Resolves.** The recall-Drift + Dormant ratified-vs-ratified conflict (the ATK's Dormant/KEEP vs the INPUT-3 reservation's Evaded/ReArm) — by naming the first principle neither encoded: the cause is unverifiable on a denominator-free rate, so the honest verdict is escalate. Behaviorally moot at v0.6 (the cell is unreachable while every class is `UnderPowered`), ruled for correctness so the cell is right the day it first becomes reachable.

---

## ADR-065 Amendment 2 (2026-06-18, honest-scope finding — Finding B) — the FULL regime's `δ'=δ/ln n` is justified by "O(log n) bucket-boundary cutpoints," but the shipped scan checks all `n−1` cuts and the `ExpHistogram` is never on the detection path: a doc-over-code over-claim (antigen's own `DocClaimVsCodeImplementationMismatch`), latent-not-live

**Status**: **DOCUMENTED finding, RULED honest-scope** (not a live-bug fix). Append-only; the §Decision and the §math contract stand as scoped here. The math-direction resolution (below) is **flagged for a build pass** (first-principles / research review), not silently picked by this note.

**What this amendment is.** Antigen's own discipline is *"the ADR says what the code does, or one of them is a finding"* (the `DocClaimVsCodeImplementationMismatch` / `RatifiedSpecDriftFromImpl` class). Applied INWARD to ADR-065's own ADWIN math, one claim does not match the shipped code. Naming it is the dogfood; this is the doc half of the v0.6 docs-currency sweep.

**The claim (CODE-TRUE on `0.6-dev`, HEAD `3f5b3b3` at finding time).** ADR-065 §"The source-verified math" and the `adwin.rs` docstrings state the FULL (variance-aware ADWIN2) regime's confidence correction is `δ' = δ/ln n` *because* "only **O(log n) bucket-boundary cutpoints** are checked" (`eps_cut_full`, `adwin.rs:205–206`; the §math bullet "FULL uses `δ'=δ/ln n` (O(log n) bucket-boundary cuts)"; `learn/adwin.rs` module doc line 41; `learn/mod.rs`). The justification is COUPLED to the cutpoint set: `δ/ln n` is the union-bound (Bonferroni) correction valid *only if* ~`ln n` cutpoints are tested — the ExpHistogram's bucket boundaries.

**What the shipped code actually does.**

- **The scan checks all `n−1` cuts, not O(log n).** `best_split` (`adwin.rs:354`, the only cut-search the detector uses) is `for cut in 1..n` over the **raw stream slice** — every interior split. Its own doc-comment says so verbatim: *"Scan all O(n) interior splits."* Both the floor and the full bound are evaluated at these all-`n−1` cuts (via `combined_eps_cut`).
- **`ExpHistogram` is built and self-tested, but is NOT on the detection path.** `pub struct ExpHistogram` (`adwin.rs:436`) is real, correct, and proven — `atk_boundary_exp_histogram_m5_conserves_under_long_chain` feeds it 1000 inserts and asserts content/capacity conservation + the M=5 bucket invariants + O(M·log n) memory. But `detect` → `detect_axis` → `detect_recursive` → `combined_eps_cut` → `best_split` never constructs or consults it. The "O(log n) bucket-boundary cutpoints" the `δ/ln n` correction is justified by are **never the cutpoints actually checked**. The EH is shipped infrastructure with no caller in the detection path.

**Why this is a latent soundness scope, not just a label mismatch.** `δ/ln n > δ/n` (since `ln n < n`), so `eps_cut_full` is *smaller* than a correctly all-cut-corrected bound would be. Because `combined_eps_cut` takes `min(floor, full)`, when the full leg wins the min it can pull the firing bound below the rigorous all-`n` floor — i.e. the FP guarantee's union-bound is under-corrected for the number of cuts actually tested. The honest correction for an all-`n−1`-cut scan is `δ/n` (the floor's), not `δ/ln n`.

**Why it is NOT a live bug today (the reason this is DOCUMENTED, not hotfixed).** The full leg is gated behind `n ≥ NORMAL_APPROX_MIN` (= 30) in `combined_eps_cut` (`adwin.rs:744`). At antigen's v0.6 scale every class is `UnderPowered` at n≈4–8 (the floor's dead-zone, ADR-065 T1), so **`eps_cut_full` is never entered in any reachable production path** — the rigorous floor (`δ'=δ/n`, all-`n` splits, Theorem-3.1-rigorous) governs every case the detector can actually reach. RUN-verified at this branch's state: the ADWIN ATK suite (`atk_adwin_boundaries`, `atk_adwin_drift_detector`, `atk_adwin_fusion_conservatism_join` — 37 tests at finding time, a count the active fixture work may grow) passes green, including a 400-point stationary-noise negative control and a 2000-point sawtooth, with **zero false fires**. (Re-run the three `atk_adwin_*` suites on your checkout for the current count.) The discrepancy is between the math's *stated justification* and the *shipped cut-set*; it does not (yet) misfire because the under-corrected leg is unreachable behind the power gate.

**The decision (what this amendment rules, and what it defers).** The honest-scope is ruled here; the math-direction is flagged for a build pass.

- **RULED (honest-scope, doc-side, no code behavior change):** the FULL regime's `δ'=δ/ln n` may NOT be justified by "O(log n) bucket-boundary cutpoints" while the shipped scan checks all `n−1`. The docs that carry the unqualified coupling claim (`adwin.rs:205–206`, the module doc, `mod.rs`, ADR-065 §math, and `roadmap.md`) are scoped to say: *the detector scans all `n−1` interior cuts; the `δ/ln n` full bound and the `ExpHistogram` window-structure are the paper's ADWIN2 form, present in the crate but not on the current all-cut detection path; the rigorous all-`n` floor (`δ/n`) governs every case the v0.6-scale detector reaches.* The EH is **reserved infrastructure**, not the live cutpoint source.
- **FLAGGED for a build pass (math-direction — NOT picked here; needs a first-principles + research review):** two sound resolutions exist and the choice is a math/architecture call, not a docs call —
  - **(A) Code → doc:** restrict the FULL-regime scan to the `ExpHistogram`'s O(log n) bucket boundaries (wire EH onto the detection path); then `δ/ln n` is correctly union-bound-corrected and the doc becomes true as written. Buys the EH's sensitivity; costs the all-cut localization the batch recursion currently uses.
  - **(B) Doc → code:** keep the all-`n−1`-cut scan and re-derive the FULL bound's correction as `δ' = δ/n` (matching the floor's cut-set), demoting `δ/ln n`; the `ExpHistogram` stays reserved future-work for a streaming/windowed variant. Keeps today's localization; loses the EH-sensitivity the `δ/ln n` term was buying.
  A born-red test should pin whichever direction is ratified (parallel to ADR-065's existing "each regime uses its own `δ'`" born-red).

**The doc changes this amendment ratifies (supersede-not-erase; docs-side only — no detector behavior changes).**

- `antigen/src/learn/adwin.rs`: the `eps_cut_full` docstring (`:205–206`) and the module-doc floor→full bullet (`:40–41`) gain the scope qualifier — the `δ/ln n` + "O(log n) bucket-cuts" line is marked as the paper's ADWIN2 form, with a note that the shipped `detect` scans all `n−1` cuts via `best_split` and the floor governs at v0.6 scale; a `// FINDING (ADR-065 Amd 2)` anchor points here.
- `antigen/src/learn/mod.rs`: the "(v0.4)" learning-core header and the B/C-only summary are refreshed to name the v0.6 organs (the same staleness this sweep fixes in `roadmap.md`); no `δ` claim lives here to scope, but the anchor is added where the regimes are summarized.
- `docs/roadmap.md` and `docs/decisions.md` (ADR-065 §math bullet "FULL uses `δ'=δ/ln n` (O(log n) bucket-boundary cuts)"): the bullet is left intact as the paper's form and annotated with a pointer to this amendment for the shipped-scan scope.

**Supersedes.** Nothing — it SCOPES (does not retract) ADR-065 §"The δ' correction is COUPLED to the cutpoint set (T3)" and §"The exponential-histogram bucket-merge (§3.3)": both remain the source-verified Bifet-Gavaldà math; this amendment records that the SHIPPED detection path realizes the floor regime's all-`n` cut-set, not the full regime's O(log n) one, so the coupling claim describes the paper, not the current `detect`.

**Resolves.** The doc-over-code over-claim surfaced by the v0.6 docs-currency sweep — antigen's own `DocClaimVsCodeImplementationMismatch` turned inward on the ADWIN organ. Latent (unreachable behind the `n ≥ 30` power gate while every class is `UnderPowered`), ruled for honest-scope now so the doc stops claiming a cutpoint-set the code does not use, with the math-direction fix flagged for the build wave before the full regime first becomes reachable.
